top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The world wakes up jittery. Sunday's drone hit on the perimeter of the UAE's Barakah nuclear plant — no leak, but a serious crack in the US-Iran ceasefire — has pushed oil prices higher again. Ukraine pulled off one of its biggest drone raids on Moscow over the weekend, killing four, and Russia answered with a heavy overnight barrage. Israeli strikes killed five in southern Lebanon despite a freshly extended ceasefire, and New York commuters are now on Day 4 without the LIRR as union talks drag on.

stories.headline: Drone strike sparks fire at UAE's Barakah nuclear plant
stories.summary: Three drones crossed the UAE's western border on Sunday. Two were shot down, but one hit an electrical generator just outside the perimeter of the Barakah nuclear power plant near Abu Dhabi, starting a fire. No radiation leaked and reactor units kept running, but the UAE called it a 'treacherous terrorist attack' and the IAEA urged maximum restraint near nuclear sites. The strike landed as the US-Iran ceasefire over the Strait of Hormuz frays.

stories.headline: Ukraine launches one of its largest drone strikes ever on Moscow region
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones flew more than 500 km overnight on Sunday to hit Moscow, killing at least four people including a woman in Khimki and two men in a village north of the capital. About a dozen more were wounded near a Moscow oil refinery. Russian air defences claim to have shot down 81 drones — one of the boldest Ukrainian raids of the war so far.

stories.headline: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 45 days — then 5 killed in south Lebanon
stories.summary: Washington announced a 45-day extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire after State Department talks. Within hours, Israeli air strikes hit several southern Lebanese towns and Baalbek in the east, killing at least five people. A formal US-facilitated security track is meant to start on May 29.

stories.headline: WHO declares Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in DRC and Uganda a global emergency
stories.summary: On Saturday the WHO Director-General declared the Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. DRC has reported 10 confirmed cases, 336 suspected, and 88 deaths across nine health zones in Ituri Province; Uganda has 2 confirmed cases including one death in Kampala. There is no approved vaccine for this Ebola strain.

stories.headline: Long Island Rail Road strike enters Day 4 — 330,000 commuters stranded
stories.summary: LIRR workers walked off the job at 12:01 a.m. on Saturday after the MTA and five unions failed to agree on a new contract. It's the first LIRR strike in 32 years and is hitting one of North America's busiest commuter lines. The unions are pushing for a 5% raise; the MTA is offering 3% with conditional add-ons. Talks resumed but no deal yet by Tuesday morning.

stories.headline: Oil jumps as Iran tensions, UAE strike rattle markets
stories.summary: Brent crude climbed near $108 a barrel and US WTI near $102 after the UAE drone incident and renewed Trump-Iran sabre-rattling. Both benchmarks are still up more than 45% since the Iran war started on Feb 28. Asian stocks opened mixed; US futures pointed lower ahead of a busy earnings week.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A quiet but practical Tuesday in Singapore. Expect short afternoon thundery showers most days through the end of May — keep an umbrella handy. The Communicable Diseases Agency confirms the two residents possibly exposed on the hantavirus-hit MV Hondius have tested negative and remain in precautionary quarantine. On the diplomatic front, Foreign Minister Vivian Balakrishnan is pushing a renewed Singapore-Johor-Riau partnership with Indonesia and Malaysia, and the next COE bidding round closes mid-week with prices still near record highs.

stories.headline: Thundery showers expected on most afternoons through end of May
stories.summary: NEA's fortnight outlook says short thundery showers will hit parts of the island on most afternoons for the rest of May, sometimes stretching into the evening. Sumatra squalls could also bring heavier rain and gusty winds in the pre-dawn and morning hours on a few days. Daily highs of 33-35°C are expected to continue.

stories.headline: Two Singapore residents on hantavirus-hit cruise ship test negative
stories.summary: The Communicable Diseases Agency confirmed that the two men (aged 65 and 67) isolated at NCID after possible exposure to the MV Hondius outbreak tested negative for hantavirus, including the Andes virus. They will stay in quarantine for 30 days from last exposure, then 15 more days of phone monitoring. The CDA says risk to the public remains low.

stories.headline: Singapore, Indonesia push to revive SIJORI partnership with Malaysia
stories.summary: Foreign Minister Vivian Balakrishnan said after his three-day Indonesia visit that Singapore and Jakarta want to bring Malaysia into a renewed Singapore-Johor-Riau Islands (SIJORI) growth triangle. The minister also flagged ASEAN Power Grid investments and digital connectivity projects as priorities amid global uncertainty.

stories.headline: Three men charged for using deregistered vehicles in LTA crackdown
stories.summary: Three men — two Singaporeans and one Singapore PR — will be charged for using deregistered vehicles in separate cases. LTA says it has investigated over 122 such cases since January 2026 and seized 55 vehicles. A 56-year-old was charged on May 14; the other two appear in court on June 4.

stories.headline: COE prices stay near record highs heading into next bidding round
stories.summary: After the first May exercise saw Cat A close at S$124,790 (up for a sixth straight round) and Cat B at S$126,236, LTA is reminding buyers to 'bid prudently' before the next round closing this week. LTA cuts Cat A quota by 2% but boosts Cat B by 7% for the May-July tender period, citing demand patterns.

stories.headline: ASEAN leaders quietly drafted an evacuation playbook for Middle East workers
stories.summary: At the ASEAN summit in Cebu earlier this month, leaders including Singapore reportedly worked on a contingency plan to evacuate over a million Southeast Asian workers from the Middle East if Iran-related hostilities reignite. Singapore officials have called the Strait of Hormuz disruption a 'dry run' for a possible US-China crisis closer to home.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All three active theatres got hotter, not cooler, over the weekend. Ukraine landed one of its biggest strikes ever on Moscow; Russia retaliated with a heavy overnight drone-and-missile barrage on Ukraine. In the Gulf, a drone hit the UAE's Barakah nuclear plant — the first attack on Emirati territory since the Iran war ceasefire. And in Lebanon, a freshly extended ceasefire is already being broken by Israeli air strikes that killed five civilians in the south.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Escalating — major mutual strikes; US push for a June peace deadline ongoing

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
Trump-brokered talks intensify; Zelensky says a 20-point peace framework is '90% agreed' but Donbas remains a sticking point.
Russia and Ukraine declare separate partial ceasefires around the WWII anniversary; they quickly fray.
Putin publicly hints he may end the war; Kremlin still rejects current US proposal as 'absolutely unacceptable' on several points.
US grants Russia a second oil-sanctions waiver, frustrating Kyiv; Belarus begins joint nuclear drills with Russia.
One of the largest Ukrainian drone attacks of the war: 4 killed (3 near Moscow, 1 in Belgorod), 12 wounded near a Moscow oil refinery; 81 drones shot down by Russian defences.

Russia retaliates with a combined drone-and-missile attack on central and southern Ukraine; reports say 1,220 Russian troops killed in 24 hours and Ukrainian positions in northern Pokrovsk are 'almost cut off'.

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran Conflict (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Fragile ceasefire under heavy strain; first UAE nuclear plant attack of the conflict

timeline.event: US and Israel launch coordinated airstrikes that kill Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei — war begins.
Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'closed', triggering a global oil-price shock and Asian fuel shortages.
Conditional US-Iran ceasefire pauses hostilities; Israel-Lebanon track also opens.
US launches counter-blockade of the Strait, targeting any ship attempting to reach Iranian ports.
Operation Epic Fury, the joint US-Israeli air campaign, is officially concluded — but Iran-Israel proxy clashes continue.
Brent and WTI rise sharply as Trump calls the ceasefire 'unbelievably weak' and Iran's offers 'garbage'.

Three drones cross the UAE's western border; two are intercepted, one hits a generator near Barakah nuclear plant, sparking a fire. UAE calls it a 'treacherous terrorist attack'. IAEA condemns. No radiation leak.

theatres.name: Israel-Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Ceasefire extended 45 days but strikes continue; Hezbollah opposes direct talks

timeline.event: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins after months of cross-border war — but is repeatedly violated from the start.
Israeli forces kill 24 across Lebanon as Tehran's response to a US deal is awaited.
Israeli airstrike on Harouf in southern Lebanon kills at least six including three paramedics; Israel says strike targeted Hamas commander Izz al-Din al-Haddad.
US State Department announces a 45-day ceasefire extension after Washington talks; a US-facilitated security track to begin May 29.
Israeli airstrikes hit Tayr Felsay, Tayr Debba, Az-Zrariyah, Jebchit and an apartment building in Baalbek — at least 5 killed. A Hezbollah legislator calls direct talks a 'dead-end path'.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius outbreak keeps spreading slowly across borders. As of May 17 there are 12 cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable, 1 inconclusive) and 3 deaths, with former passengers now hospitalised or quarantined across 12+ countries on five continents. Singapore's two suspected residents tested negative. WHO still rates the wider epidemic risk as low — Andes virus only spreads through close, sustained contact — but with a 20-40% case fatality rate, every former passenger is being tracked.

stories.headline: ECDC update: 12 cases, 3 deaths in MV Hondius cluster
stories.summary: As of May 17, ECDC tallies 9 confirmed, 2 probable, and 1 inconclusive case across the MV Hondius cluster, with 3 deaths (2 confirmed Andes virus). Former passengers and crew are being followed up in Australia, Canada, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Saint Helena, Singapore, South Africa, Spain, Switzerland, Turkey and the US.

stories.headline: CDC classifies Hondius response as 'Level 3' emergency
stories.summary: The US Centers for Disease Control has bumped its Hondius hantavirus response to a 'Level 3' emergency activation, mobilising staff for contact tracing of US-based former passengers. The CDC says no US deaths so far but stresses that Andes virus is the only hantavirus known to spread person-to-person, possibly via airborne particles.

stories.headline: Both Singapore residents test negative; remain in 30-day quarantine
stories.summary: Singapore's CDA confirmed that the two residents isolated at NCID (aged 65 and 67) tested negative for hantavirus and the Andes virus. They will stay in quarantine for 30 days from last exposure, then 15 more days of phone monitoring covering the 45-day incubation window. Public-risk level remains low.

stories.headline: WHO ships 2,500 diagnostic kits, deploys onboard expert
stories.summary: WHO's latest situation report says 2,500 diagnostic kits have been dispatched from Argentina to laboratories across five countries to speed testing. An expert epidemiologist has been deployed onboard the Hondius. The organisation continues to assess the wider epidemic risk as low but the affected-passenger risk as high.

stories.headline: New global health emergency: Ebola Bundibugyo in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Separately from the Hondius cluster, WHO has declared the Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. As of May 18 there are 10 confirmed and 336 suspected cases in DRC (88 suspected deaths) plus 2 confirmed cases in Uganda. No approved vaccine for this strain exists.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Defenders had a heavy weekend. CISA's deadline to patch a critical Cisco SD-WAN authentication-bypass (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) hit on May 17 — assume it's being weaponised against any unpatched controller. An 18-year-old NGINX heap-overflow (CVE-2026-42945, CVSS 9.2) is being actively exploited. On top of that, two unpatched Windows zero-days — YellowKey (BitLocker bypass) and GreenPlasma (privilege escalation) — were dumped publicly by a frustrated researcher. Patch, monitor and prepare for follow-on attacks.

stories.headline: Cisco SD-WAN auth bypass — federal patch deadline hit on May 17
stories.summary: A critical authentication-bypass flaw in Cisco's Catalyst SD-WAN Controller is being used in zero-day attacks to gain administrative privileges on compromised devices. CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalogue and ordered federal agencies to patch by Sunday May 17. Private-sector defenders should treat it as urgent.

stories.headline: 18-year-old NGINX heap overflow under active exploitation
stories.summary: A heap buffer overflow in NGINX's ngx_http_rewrite_module — present in versions 0.6.27 through 1.30.0, so most production NGINX deployments — can cause denial-of-service and potentially remote code execution. The bug has been silently sitting in the codebase for roughly 18 years. NGINX Plus and Open Source builds are both affected; patch now.

stories.headline: Two Windows zero-days dropped publicly: BitLocker bypass and SYSTEM privilege escalation
stories.summary: Researcher 'Chaotic Eclipse', frustrated with Microsoft's handling of bug reports, dropped two new Windows zero-days dubbed YellowKey and GreenPlasma. YellowKey lets an attacker with physical access bypass BitLocker on Windows 11/Server 2022-2025 via a USB-staged exploit in WinRE. GreenPlasma is a local privilege escalation via the CTFMON process that gives SYSTEM-level access. No patch yet.

stories.headline: CISA adds Linux 'Copy Fail' kernel privilege escalation to KEV
stories.summary: CISA added a recently disclosed Linux kernel local privilege escalation bug to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue, citing in-the-wild abuse. Unprivileged local users can chain it to become root across multiple Linux distributions. FCEB agencies were told to remediate by May 15; everyone else should patch immediately.

stories.headline: AI-generated 2FA bypass zero-day disrupted by Google researchers
stories.summary: Google's Threat Intelligence team says it likely thwarted what could have been the first 'mass exploitation event' powered by an AI-built zero-day. The attacker used a large language model to develop a Python exploit that bypasses two-factor authentication on a popular open-source web admin tool. The tooling, code style and lifecycle showed strong LLM fingerprints — and a preview of what's coming.

stories.headline: Pwn2Own Berlin 2026: $1.29M paid for 47 zero-days; DEVCORE wins Master of Pwn
stories.summary: Three days of competition produced 47 brand-new zero-day exploits against Windows 11, Microsoft Edge, SharePoint, VMware ESXi, Red Hat Enterprise Linux and AI tools including LiteLLM. STARLabs' Nguyen Hoang Thach earned $200K for a cross-tenant code-execution exploit against VMware ESXi. DEVCORE took the Master of Pwn title.
